title: Preslianthus panamensis
intro: Preslianthus panamensis, also known as Panama preslia, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The health benefits of Preslianthus panamensis include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, colds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Preslianthus panamensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to make teas and tinctures.
taxonomy: Preslianthus panamensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to Central and South America, specifically in Costa Rica, Panama, and Colombia. Preslianthus panamensis is classified in the genus Preslianthus, which is a part of the order Lamiales. Further classification places it in the family Rubiaceae, which includes over 13,000 species of plants. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and shared characteristics with other members of the Rubiaceae family.
